implementation_plan.overview: Add video file import (MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, WebM, FLV, WMV) by creating AudioExtractionService (wrapping fluent-ffmpeg with ffmpeg-static), VideoConverter (IConverter implementation mirroring AudioConverter), and extending the transcription pipeline with two-phase progress (extraction 0-20%, transcription 20-100%). Video files route through TranscriptionDialog for language selection, same as audio.

steps.description: Install npm dependencies: fluent-ffmpeg, @types/fluent-ffmpeg, ffmpeg-static, ffprobe-static
steps.rationale: Bundled ffmpeg binaries avoid requiring system ffmpeg installation. fluent-ffmpeg provides stable Node.js API.

steps.description: Add VIDEO_IMPORT constants (SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS, EXTRACTION_TIMEOUT_MS, TEMP_PREFIX, EXTRACTION_PROGRESS_WEIGHT) and VIDEO_* error codes (VIDEO_NO_AUDIO, VIDEO_EXTRACTION_FAILED, VIDEO_FFMPEG_MISSING) with user-friendly messages
steps.rationale: Centralized constants and error codes follow existing patterns (TRANSCRIPTION, SCREENSHOT blocks in constants.ts, ErrorCode enum in errors.ts)

steps.description: Create AudioExtractionService with hasAudioStream(), extractAudio() (to temp WAV with progress/abort), getVideoMetadata() (resolution/codec), and isAvailable() static check. Include singleton export and factory function.
steps.rationale: Isolated service for ffmpeg operations, testable with mocked fluent-ffmpeg. Follows service pattern (singleton + factory) used by AudioMetadataService.

steps.description: Add VIDEO_EXTENSIONS constant and isVideoExtension() helper to extensions.ts
steps.rationale: Mirrors existing isTextExtension()/isCodeExtension() pattern for file type detection

steps.description: Create VideoConverter implementing IConverter with category 'video', DI constructor accepting transcriptionService, audioMetadataService, audioExtractionService. validate() delegates to validateFileForImport(). convert() extracts audio, transcribes, formats markdown with video-specific frontmatter (type, resolution, codec).
steps.rationale: Exact mirror of AudioConverter pattern with additional extraction step and video-specific frontmatter

steps.description: Register VideoConverter in ConverterRegistry.registerBuiltInConverters() and export from import/index.ts
steps.rationale: Makes video extensions available in registry for file dialog and import routing

steps.description: Extend transcription-handlers.ts: detect video extensions in import handler, call hasAudioStream() (error if none), extractAudio() with progress mapped to 0-20%, transcribe extracted WAV with progress 20-100%, add video-specific frontmatter fields, clean up temp WAV in finally block. Extend validate handler for video files using ffprobe.
steps.rationale: The interactive import path (with progress streaming) runs through transcription-handlers, not VideoConverter.convert(). Two-phase progress requires mapping extraction and transcription phases.

steps.description: Add 'Video Files' filter group in import-handlers.ts file dialog
steps.rationale: Separate filter group for video (like audio has its own group) for clear file dialog UX

steps.description: Extend useImport.ts: add isVideoFile() function using VIDEO_IMPORT.SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS, route video files to TranscriptionDialog in importFile(), filter video files from batch imports in processFiles(), update warning messages to include 'video'
steps.rationale: Video files need same interactive flow as audio -- language selection dialog, progress, etc.

steps.description: Update TranscriptionDialog: show 'Transcribe video' title and FileVideo icon when file has video extension. No structural changes needed -- progress phases come from backend.
steps.rationale: Visual distinction for video vs audio import. Minimal change since the dialog is already file-type agnostic.

patterns_to_follow: Service pattern with singleton + factory function (AudioMetadataService style)
DI via constructor with interface types (ITranscriptionServiceLike pattern)
Constants block in shared/constants.ts (TRANSCRIPTION, SCREENSHOT blocks)
ErrorCode enum entries with ERROR_MESSAGES mapping
Vitest with vi.mock() for dependency isolation
JSDoc comments on all public functions
patterns_to_avoid: Adding video-specific state to useTranscriptionStore (keep it file-type agnostic)
Hardcoded extension lists (use constants from shared/constants.ts)

file_changes.description: Add VIDEO_NO_AUDIO, VIDEO_EXTRACTION_FAILED, VIDEO_FFMPEG_MISSING error codes and messages
ffmpeg wrapper service for audio extraction, audio stream detection, and video metadata
Add VIDEO_EXTENSIONS constant and isVideoExtension() helper
IConverter implementation for video files with audio extraction and transcription
Import and register VideoConverter in registerBuiltInConverters()
Add video detection, audio extraction with two-phase progress, video frontmatter, temp cleanup, video validation
Add Video Files filter group to file dialog
Add isVideoFile(), route video to TranscriptionDialog, filter from batch
Show video-specific title and icon when importing video file

key_scenarios: AudioExtractionService.hasAudioStream returns true for video with audio
AudioExtractionService.hasAudioStream returns false for video without audio
AudioExtractionService.extractAudio produces temp WAV and returns duration
AudioExtractionService.extractAudio handles abort signal
AudioExtractionService.extractAudio cleans up on failure
AudioExtractionService.getVideoMetadata returns resolution and codec
AudioExtractionService.getVideoMetadata returns null on probe failure
AudioExtractionService.isAvailable returns false when ffmpeg-static missing
VideoConverter has correct supportedExtensions, requiresConversion, category
VideoConverter.validate delegates to validateFileForImport
VideoConverter.convert success produces markdown with video frontmatter
VideoConverter.convert fails with clear error when no audio stream
VideoConverter.convert fails with clear error when extraction fails
VideoConverter.convert includes resolution and codec in frontmatter when available
VideoConverter.convert omits resolution/codec when metadata unavailable
isVideoFile correctly identifies video extensions
Video files route to TranscriptionDialog in useImport
Video files rejected from batch imports with warning
Two-phase progress maps extraction to 0-20% and transcription to 20-100%
Temp extracted WAV cleaned up after success
Temp extracted WAV cleaned up after failure

risks.risk: ffmpeg-static binary significantly increases app bundle size (~70-100MB per platform)
risks.mitigation: Measure bundle size impact before merging. Consider optional dependency or lazy download pattern if too large. Document size increase in PR.

risks.risk: fluent-ffmpeg progress reporting inconsistency across video codecs
risks.mitigation: Use timemark-based progress calculation as fallback when percent is unavailable. Progress is UX-only, not correctness-critical.

risks.risk: Video files without audio streams cause confusing user experience
risks.mitigation: Explicit hasAudioStream() check before extraction with clear error message: 'This video has no audio track to transcribe.'

risks.risk: Large video files (>1GB) cause long extraction times or temp disk space issues
risks.mitigation: Extraction timeout (5 min), AbortSignal support, WAV output is mono 16kHz (smaller than source). Could add size warning for very large files.

risks.risk: Electron ASAR packaging conflicts with ffmpeg-static binary paths
risks.mitigation: ffmpeg-static and ffprobe-static binaries must be excluded from ASAR. Check electron-builder config asarUnpack patterns.

verification_criteria: VideoConverter registered and accessible via converterRegistry.getConverter('mp4')
Video extensions appear in file dialog filters
Video file selected via dialog opens TranscriptionDialog with video title
Video with audio: extraction produces temp WAV, transcription produces markdown with video frontmatter
Video without audio: clear error message before transcription attempt
Temp WAV files cleaned up after success and failure
Video files rejected from batch drop with warning toast
All existing audio import tests still pass
npm run test passes with >80% coverage on new files
